window_summary: This window tracks the recent pallets/flask release stream from 1.1.1 through 3.1.3, covering major feature releases, bug fixes, and security patches. It highlights the evolution of the framework including the 2.0.0 major release, the 3.0.0 feature release, and subsequent 3.1.x updates.

Tag: 1.1.1; draft: False; prerelease: False; published_at: 2019-07-09T14:57:52Z; created_at: 2019-07-08T17:58:12Z

* Changelog: https://flask.palletsprojects.com/en/1.1.x/changelog/#version-1-1-1

chunks.raw_text:
Repository: pallets/flask
Release: 1.1.2
Tag: 1.1.2; draft: False; prerelease: False; published_at: 2020-04-03T17:22:22Z; created_at: 2020-04-03T17:10:38Z
Release notes:
1.1.x is the last version to support Python 2.7 and Python 3.5. It also contains deprecation warnings for code that will be removed in 2.0. Please pay attention to deprecation warnings in your project!
This release contains a couple bug fixes.
* Changelog: https://flask.palletsprojects.com/en/1.1.x/changelog/#version-1-1-2
chunks.memory_unit_text: In the pallets/flask release 1.1.2, the project notes that 1.1.x is the last version to support Python 2.7 and Python 3.5, contains deprecation warnings for code that will be removed in 2.0, and includes a couple bug fixes.

chunks.raw_text:
Repository: pallets/flask
Release: 2.0.0
Tag: 2.0.0; draft: False; prerelease: False; published_at: 2021-05-12T00:11:51Z; created_at: 2021-05-11T21:44:16Z
Release notes:
New major versions of all the core Pallets libraries, including Flask 2.0, have been released! :tada:
* Read the announcement on our blog: https://palletsprojects.com/blog/flask-2-0-released/
* Read the full list of changes: https://flask.palletsprojects.com/changes/#version-2-0-0
* Retweet the announcement on Twitter: https://twitter.com/PalletsTeam/status/1392266507296514048
* Follow our blog, Twitter, or GitHub to see future announcements.
This represents a significant amount of work, and there are quite a few changes. Be sure to carefully read the changelog, and use tools such as pip-compile and Dependabot to pin your dependencies and control your updates.
chunks.memory_unit_text: In the pallets/flask release 2.0.0, the project announces new major versions of all core Pallets libraries including Flask 2.0, with a significant amount of work and many changes, and advises careful reading of the changelog and using tools like pip-compile and Dependabot to pin dependencies.

chunks.raw_text:
Repository: pallets/flask
Release: 2.1.0
Tag: 2.1.0; draft: False; prerelease: False; published_at: 2022-03-28T19:21:43Z; created_at: 2022-03-28T19:12:29Z
Release notes:
This is a feature release, which includes new features and removes previously deprecated features. The 2.1.x branch is now the supported bugfix branch, the 2.0.x branch will become a tag marking the end of support for that branch. We encourage everyone to upgrade, and to use a tool such as [pip-tools](https://pypi.org/project/pip-tools/) to pin all dependencies and control upgrades.
* Changes: https://flask.palletsprojects.com/en/2.1.x/changes/#version-2-1-0
* Milestone: https://github.com/pallets/flask/milestone/13?closed=1
We also encourage upgrading to the latest versions of the other Pallets projects as well.
* Werkzeug 2.1 changes: https://werkzeug.palletsprojects.com/en/2.1.x/changes/#version-2-1-0
* Jinja 3.1 changes: https://jinja.palletsprojects.com/en/3.1.x/changes/#version-3-1-1
* Click 8.1 changes: https://click.palletsprojects.com/en/8.1.x/changes/#version-8-1-0
* MarkupSafe 2.1 changes: https://markupsafe.palletsprojects.com/en/2.1.x/changes/#version-2-1-1
* ItsDangerous 2.1 changes: https://itsdangerous.palletsprojects.com/en/2.1.x/changes/#version-2-1-2
chunks.memory_unit_text: In the pallets/flask release 2.1.0, the project announces a feature release that includes new features and removes previously deprecated features, makes the 2.1.x branch the supported bugfix branch, and encourages upgrading along with other Pallets projects like Werkzeug 2.1, Jinja 3.1, Click 8.1, MarkupSafe 2.1, and ItsDangerous 2.1.

Repository: pallets/flask
Release: 3.0.1
Tag: 3.0.1; draft: False; prerelease: False; published_at: 2024-01-18T20:03:56Z; created_at: 2024-01-18T19:57:54Z
Release notes:
This is a fix release for the 3.0.x feature release branch.
Fixes an issue where using other JSON providers, such as `flask-orjson`, previously caused loaded session data to have an incorrect format in some cases.
* Changes: https://flask.palletsprojects.com/en/3.0.x/changes/#version-3-0-1
* Milestone: https://github.com/pallets/flask/milestone/32?closed=1

chunks.raw_text:
Repository: pallets/flask
Release: 3.0.3
Tag: 3.0.3; draft: False; prerelease: False; published_at: 2024-04-07T19:27:16Z; created_at: 2024-04-07T19:19:46Z
Release notes:
This is a fix release for the 3.0.x feature branch.
PyPI: https://pypi.org/project/Flask/3.0.3/
Changes: https://flask.palletsprojects.com/en/3.0.x/changes/#version-3-0-3
Milestone: https://github.com/pallets/flask/milestone/35?closed=1
-   The default `hashlib.sha1` may not be available in FIPS builds. Don't access it at import time so the developer has time to change the default. #5448
-   Don't initialize the `cli` attribute in the sansio scaffold, but rather in the `Flask` concrete class. #5270
chunks.memory_unit_text: In the pallets/flask release 3.0.3, the project provides a fix release for the 3.0.x feature branch, with fixes including not accessing hashlib.sha1 at import time for FIPS builds and not initializing the cli attribute in the sansio scaffold.

chunks.raw_text:
Repository: pallets/flask
Release: 3.1.0
Tag: 3.1.0; draft: False; prerelease: False; published_at: 2024-11-13T18:29:05Z; created_at: 2024-11-13T18:20:14Z
Release notes:
This is the Flask 3.1.0 feature release. A feature release may include new features, remove previously deprecated code, add new deprecations, or introduce potentially breaking changes. We encourage everyone to upgrade, and to use a tool such as [pip-tools](https://pypi.org/project/pip-tools/) to pin all dependencies and control upgrades. Test with warnings treated as errors to be able to adapt to deprecation warnings early.
PyPI: https://pypi.org/project/Flask/3.1.0/
Changes: https://flask.palletsprojects.com/en/stable/changes/#version-3-1-0
Milestone: https://github.com/pallets/flask/milestone/33?closed=1
- Drop support for Python 3.8. #5623
- Update minimum dependency versions to latest feature releases. Werkzeug >= 3.1, ItsDangerous >= 2.2, Blinker >= 1.9. #5624, #5633
- Provide a configuration option to control automatic option responses. #5496
- `Flask.open_resource`/`open_instance_resource` and `Blueprint.open_resource` take an `encoding` parameter to use when opening in text mode. It defaults to `utf-8`. #5504
- `Request.max_content_length` can be customized per-request instead of only through the `MAX_CONTENT_LENGTH` config. Added `MAX_FORM_MEMORY_SIZE` and `MAX_FORM_PARTS` config. Added documentation about resource limits to the security page. #5625
- Add support for the `Partitioned` cookie attribute (CHIPS), with the `SESSION_COOKIE_PARTITIONED` config. #5472
- `-e path` takes precedence over default `.env` and `.flaskenv` files. `load_dotenv` loads default files in addition to a path unless `load_defaults=False` is passed. #5628
- Support key rotation with the `SECRET_KEY_FALLBACKS` config, a list of old secret keys that can still be used for unsigning. Extensions will need to add support. #5621
- Fix how setting `host_matching=True` or `subdomain_matching=False` interacts with `SERVER_NAME`. Setting `SERVER_NAME` no longer restricts requests to only that domain. #5553
- `Request.trusted_hosts` is checked during routing, and can be set through the `TRUSTED_HOSTS` config. #5636
chunks.memory_unit_text: In the pallets/flask release 3.1.0, the project announces a feature release that may include new features, removes previously deprecated code, adds new deprecations, or introduces potentially breaking changes. It drops support for Python 3.8, updates minimum dependency versions to Werkzeug >= 3.1, ItsDangerous >= 2.2, Blinker >= 1.9, provides a configuration option to control automatic option responses, adds encoding parameter to open_resource methods, allows per-request customization of max_content_length, adds support for Partitioned cookie attribute, supports key rotation with SECRET_KEY_FALLBACKS, fixes host_matching and subdomain_matching interaction with SERVER_NAME, and checks Request.trusted_hosts during routing.

chunks.raw_text:
Repository: pallets/flask
Release: 3.1.1
Tag: 3.1.1; draft: False; prerelease: False; published_at: 2025-05-13T15:01:32Z; created_at: 2025-05-13T14:53:02Z
Release notes:
This is the Flask 3.1.1 fix release, which fixes bugs but does not otherwise change behavior and should not result in breaking changes compared to the latest feature release.
PyPI: https://pypi.org/project/Flask/3.1.1/
Changes: https://flask.palletsprojects.com/en/stable/changes/#version-3-1-1
Milestone https://github.com/pallets/flask/milestone/36?closed=1
- Fix signing key selection order when key rotation is enabled via `SECRET_KEY_FALLBACKS`. GHSA-4grg-w6v8-c28g
- Fix type hint for `cli_runner.invoke`. #5645
- `flask --help` loads the app and plugins first to make sure all commands are shown. #5673
- Mark sans-io base class as being able to handle views that return `AsyncIterable`. This is not accurate for Flask, but makes typing easier for Quart. #5659
chunks.memory_unit_text: In the pallets/flask release 3.1.1, the project provides a fix release that fixes bugs but does not otherwise change behavior. It fixes signing key selection order when key rotation is enabled via SECRET_KEY_FALLBACKS (GHSA-4grg-w6v8-c28g), fixes type hint for cli_runner.invoke, ensures flask --help loads the app and plugins first, and marks sans-io base class as being able to handle views that return AsyncIterable.

This is the Flask 3.1.2 fix release, which fixes bugs but does not otherwise change behavior and should not result in breaking changes compared to the latest feature release.
PyPI: https://pypi.org/project/Flask/3.1.2/
Changes: https://flask.palletsprojects.com/page/changes/#version-3-1-2
Milestone: https://github.com/pallets/flask/milestone/38?closed=1
- `stream_with_context` does not fail inside async views. #5774
- When using `follow_redirects` in the test client, the final state of `session` is correct. #5786
- Relax type hint for passing bytes IO to `send_file`. #5776
chunks.memory_unit_text: In the pallets/flask release 3.1.2, the project provides a fix release that fixes bugs but does not otherwise change behavior. It fixes stream_with_context inside async views, ensures the final state of session is correct when using follow_redirects in the test client, and relaxes type hint for passing bytes IO to send_file.

This is the Flask 3.1.3 security fix release, which fixes a security issue but does not otherwise change behavior and should not result in breaking changes compared to the latest feature release.
PyPI: https://pypi.org/project/Flask/3.1.3/
Changes: https://flask.palletsprojects.com/page/changes/#version-3-1-3
- The session is marked as accessed for operations that only access the keys but not the values, such as `in` and `len`. [GHSA-68rp-wp8r-4726](https://github.com/pallets/flask/security/advisories/GHSA-68rp-wp8r-4726)
chunks.memory_unit_text: In the pallets/flask release 3.1.3, the project provides a security fix release that fixes a security issue but does not otherwise change behavior. It marks the session as accessed for operations that only access the keys but not the values, such as in and len, referencing GHSA-68rp-wp8r-4726.

questions.query_text: In the pallets/flask releases, which release fixes an issue where using other JSON providers such as flask-orjson previously caused loaded session data to have an incorrect format in some cases?

answer_support.answer_text: fixing an issue where using other JSON providers such as flask-orjson previously caused loaded session data to have an incorrect format in some cases

questions.query_text: Across the pallets/flask releases, how does the security fix in Flask 2.3.2 differ from the security fix in Flask 3.1.3?

options.text: 2.3.2 addresses CVE-2023-30861, while 3.1.3 addresses GHSA-68rp-wp8r-4726 related to session key access.
2.3.2 fixes a JSON provider issue, while 3.1.3 fixes a type hint.
Both releases fix the same CVE-2023-30861.
2.3.2 adds key rotation support, while 3.1.3 drops Python 3.8 support.
